description: Bid 2C over 1NT to find a 4-4 major-suit fit
purpose: Discover whether opener holds a 4-card major so the partnership can play in an 8-card major fit instead of notrump

teaching.tradeoff: Using the ask as Stayman means the partnership cannot play the ask in its natural strain.
teaching.principle: Always check for a four-card major fit before settling in notrump — majors score better and play easier with a trump suit.

commonMistakes.text: Do not use Stayman with a flat hand and no ruffing value.
commonMistakes.reason: A balanced responder gets no extra tricks from the major fit, so notrump usually scores at least as well.

commonMistakes.text: Do not use Stayman when your only four-card major is the one opener has already denied.
commonMistakes.reason: No fit is possible, so the ask only gives the defenders information about responder's shape.

commonMistakes.text: Do not launch Stayman without a plan for each possible response.
commonMistakes.reason: Responder must already know which major to raise, which to sign off, and how to invite before starting the sequence.

commonMistakes.text: Do not use Stayman just to escape a weak notrump with both majors and short clubs.
commonMistakes.reason: That is a separate agreement (garbage Stayman), and treating the ask as constructive creates misunderstandings.

definitions.description: Eligible for regular Stayman (invite+ values, excluding flat one-major 4-3-3-3 hands)
Stayman preferred (eligible AND either no 5-card major OR 5-4 in both majors)
Posterior probability that N/S have an 8+ card major fit
Posterior probability that opener has balanced shape
Posterior probability that opener has a second 4-card major

summaryCard.trigger: Partner opens one notrump, you respond

whenToUse.gloss: Use regular Stayman with invitational or better values when you need the follow-up structure and are not the flat four-three-three-three one-major exception.
Use Stayman with both majors and invitational or better values so later rebids can sort out the best fit or notrump contract.

workedAuctions.label: Invitational spade hand finds a fit

calls.rationale: Opener shows a balanced 15-17 HCP hand.
Responder asks about a 4-card major with invitational values.
Opener shows 4 spades (and denies 4 hearts or promises shape per partnership style).
Responder invites game in the confirmed 4-4 spade fit; opener accepts with a maximum or declines with a minimum.

workedAuctions.label: Game-forcing heart hand lands in game

calls.rationale: Opener shows a balanced 15-17 HCP hand.
Responder asks about a 4-card major with game-forcing values.
Responder bids game in the confirmed 4-4 heart fit, preferring the major to 3NT on the same values.

workedAuctions.label: Game values with no major fit still reach notrump game

calls.rationale: Opener shows a balanced 15-17 HCP hand.
Responder checks for a 4-card major before committing to notrump game.
With game values and no major fit, responder places the contract in 3NT after checking for a better major-suit game first.

workedAuctions.label: Counter-example: flat shape steers to the wrong game

calls.rationale: Opener shows a balanced 15-17 HCP hand.
Responder asks automatically because of the 4-card heart, even though the 4-3-3-3 shape has no ruffing value.
Opener shows four hearts, so the fit exists.
Responder steers to 4♥ because the fit was found, but 3NT was cold while 4♥ needed a finesse and went down.

items.ourAction: Stayman is normally off; systems-on agreements vary, so check with partner.
items.note: Many partnerships play runouts over the double, so the ask is no longer available by default.

items.opponentAction: Overcall of two of a minor
items.ourAction: A cue of the overcalled minor typically replaces the ask.
items.note: Natural bids of two of a major become available and may be preferred with a real suit.

items.opponentAction: Overcall at two of a major or higher
items.ourAction: The ask is unavailable; bid naturally or use a partnership-specific gadget such as Lebensohl.
items.note: Interference above two of a minor preempts the room needed for the ask.

relatedLinks.discriminator: Use garbage Stayman to escape a weak hand with both majors and short clubs; standard Stayman promises invitational or better values.
Use transfers when responder has a single long major and wants opener to declare; use Stayman when responder wants the major ask or Larry's regular follow-up structure.
Prefer Smolen when responder is five-four in the majors and game-forcing; the ask still starts the auction, but Smolen lets opener declare the major fit.

teaching.purpose: Use 2♣ over 1NT to ask about majors and access Larry Cohen's regular Stayman follow-ups.
teaching.whenToUse: Partner opens 1NT (15-17 HCP balanced). You have invitational or game values and are not the flat 4-3-3-3 one-major exception; invitational or game-forcing 5-4 major hands are also legal.
whenNotToUse: Weak hand without the separate Garbage Stayman agreement
Flat 4-3-3-3 hand with only one four-card major
Single five-card major hands that belong in Jacoby Transfers
teaching.principle: 2♣ asks opener about majors, but the ask itself does not guarantee responder holds a four-card major.
teaching.roles: Responder is captain — asks opener to describe, then decides the final contract
